FenceData.title: FenceData
FenceData.description: Represents the real-time state of the electric fence in Firebase Realtime Database.

status.description: The current status of the fence (e.g., 'LEGAL', 'ILLEGAL_NO_PULSE').

source.description: Indicates if the status was set by the 'ADMIN' panel or 'AUTO'matically by the device.

gasValue.description: The gas level reading from the MQ sensor.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp of the last status update.

SensorData.title: SensorData
SensorData.description: Represents sensor data from the IoT device. This is for the original dashboard and is separate from the new Realtime Database structure.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SensorData entity.

gasValue.description: The gas level reading from the MQ sensor (e.g., in PPM).

lastRead.description: Timestamp of when sensor data was last read.

definition.description: Stores the real-time state of the electric fence. This path is in Firebase Realtime Database, not Firestore.
Stores sensor data for the original dashboard. This path is in Firestore.

params.name: sensorDataId
params.description: Unique identifier for the sensor data document.

firestore.reasoning: The application is being refactored to use Firebase Realtime Database for its core two-way data synchronization between the web app and an ESP8266 device. The primary data is stored under the `/fence` path in RTDB. The data structure is kept flat and simple for easy consumption by the microcontroller. The original Firestore structure for `sensor_data` is kept for the unmodified `/dashboard` page, while the new `/dashboard_1` and `/admin` pages will use the Realtime Database. This approach fulfills the new requirements while maintaining backward compatibility for the old dashboard.
